title: The Iron Horse

evidence.note: Published 1924; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

watch.label: Internet Archive — The Iron Horse (1924), full film

editorial: The Iron Horse is a 1924 American silent epic Western directed by John Ford and produced by Fox Film Corporation, starring George O'Brien and Madge Bellamy. Fox's large-scale answer to 'The Covered Wagon,' it dramatizes the building of the first transcontinental railroad, was the top-grossing film of 1924, and marked Ford's emergence as a major director of Westerns; it entered the National Film Registry in 2011. Published in 1924, the film print's 95-year US copyright term has expired, placing the photoplay in the public domain by term expiry.
